primary_variables.matched_definition: Comparison by phosphorylation, acetylation, ubiquitination, or activation markers.
evidence: Proteins significantly enriched in the Hyd(+) condition, compared with the control Hyd(−) condition, are considered as candidate S-acylated proteins.

The present study aimed to address the following pressing questions: (1) To what extent do ABE methods suffer from the background limitation? (2) Can we substantially reduce the background and improve the signal-to-noise ratios for enriched S-acylated proteins?

attributes.route_or_context: cleave thioester bonds to convert S-acylated proteins into biotinylated proteins
evidence: S-acyl groups are specifically removed from cysteine residues by using neutral hydroxylamine (Hyd) to cleave thioester bonds
one volume of 2 M neutralized (pH 7.4) hydroxylamine, 1 mM biotin-HPDP, 50% DMF, was added

attributes.route_or_context: additional blockage of residual free cysteine residues
evidence: The background can be substantially reduced by an additional blockage of residual free cysteine residues with 2,2′-dithiodipyridine prior to the biotin-HPDP reaction.
reaction with 25 mM DTDP was performed between NEM alkylation and biotin-HPDP reaction.

replicate_hints: three biological replicates were subjected to LB-ABE enrichment and endoprotease digestion
multiplex_channel_hints: light SILAC-labeled cells were treated with neutral hydroxylamine (Hyd)
heavy SILAC-labeled cells were treated with control Tris-HCl buffer

evidence: Proteins significantly enriched in the Hyd(+) condition, compared with the control Hyd(−) condition, are considered as candidate S-acylated proteins.
a comparison of the log2-transformed Hyd+/Hyd− ratios suggested that the LB-ABE method provided a much cleaner enrichment (i.e., higher Hyd+/Hyd− ratios) than the ABE method

quantification_strategy: stable isotope labeling by amino acids in cell culture (SILAC)

base_candidates_ranked.reason: PRIDE metadata and text explicitly state the use of SILAC (Arg10/Lys8) for quantification of ABE-enriched proteins.
The LB-ABE method was also coupled with label-free proteomics (LFQ) for the LNCaP cell profiling.

modifier_candidates_ranked.reason: The experiment uses Acyl-Biotinyl Exchange (ABE) and LB-ABE to specifically enrich S-acylated cysteine residues.
